# Prashanth Rajagopal

> Software architect building Astra, an autonomous agent OS. Blog on distributed systems, Go, Rails, IoT, electronics, and agent infrastructure. 15+ years building production software.

## Blog

- [LiteLLM and the trust chain nobody audits](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/litellm-and-the-trust-chain-nobody-audits/): A compromised security scanner led to malicious PyPI packages that harvested credentials and spread through Kubernetes clusters in three hours.
- [The 10ms lie we all tell](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/the-10ms-lie-we-all-tell/): Cache discipline as architecture discipline — every cache is a bet, and we only count the wins.
- [What Rails taught me about supervision](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/what-rails-taught-me-about-supervision/): How Puma, Sidekiq, and a decade of Rails shaped Astra's actor supervision tree.
- [Sandboxes aren't security theater](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/sandboxes-arent-security-theater/): WASM, Docker, Firecracker — choosing the right isolation when agents run untrusted tools.
- [Why Postgres stays sacred](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/why-postgres-stays-sacred/): Caches lie with TTLs, replicas lie with lag. Postgres is where the lies stop.
- [Scheduling without losing your mind](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/scheduling-without-losing-your-mind/): Heartbeats, sharded schedulers, and at-least-once delivery in distributed task scheduling.
- [Chat is the easy part](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/chat-is-the-easy-part/): What it actually took to bolt WebSocket streaming onto a task-graph agent system.
- [The kernel's first 10ms](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/kernels-first-10ms/): What belongs on the hot path between Send and Receive in an actor kernel, and what gets kicked out.
- [The approval gate nobody wants](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/the-approval-gate-nobody-wants/): Building dual-approval and plan gates into an autonomous agent system.
- [Why Astra runs on a Mac Mini](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/why-astra-runs-on-a-mac-mini/): macOS as a production deployment target — Metal acceleration, native binaries, no emulation.
- [Why I didn't use Kubernetes](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/why-i-didnt-use-kubernetes/): Docker Compose for infrastructure, native Go for services — why premature orchestration crystallizes the wrong boundaries.
- [Agent memory is not a vector database](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/agent-memory-is-not-a-vector-database/): Working memory, episodic memory, and semantic search are three different things. pgvector is one layer.
- [Dead letters are a feature, not a failure](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/dead-letters-are-a-feature/): Designing for task exhaustion with dead-letter queues and retry budgets.
- [Why I'm building Astra](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/why-im-building-astra/): Manifesto for building an OS-shaped substrate for autonomous agents.
- [My first RC car build: an engineer's guide to pain in India](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/rc-cars-and-the-india-parts-problem/): Choosing a platform, brushless ESCs, LiPo safety, and the uniquely Indian challenge of getting parts at all.
- [Ruby in 2026: still boring, still right](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/ruby-in-2026/): Rails isn't trendy and it isn't dead. Boring technology choices compound.
- [ESP32, MQTT, and the $4 sensor that replaced a $200 one](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/esp32-mqtt-and-the-4-dollar-sensor/): Building DIY IoT sensors with ESP32 and ESPHome — why commercial IoT is overpriced.
- [Home Assistant ruined my light switches](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/home-assistant-ruined-my-light-switches/): From smart bulbs to a full Home Assistant setup — MQTT, Zigbee, automations that work.
- [What electronics taught me about debugging software](https://prashanthr.net/blog/posts/what-electronics-taught-me-about-debugging/): Oscilloscopes, multimeters, and tracing signals — the hardware debugging mindset that transfers to distributed systems.

## Astra

- [Architecture overview](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/astra/architecture/overview/): Microkernel design, actor framework, task graph, scheduler, memory subsystem, LLM routing.
- [Kernel design](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/astra/architecture/kernel/): Actor runtime, task graphs, scheduling, messaging, P0-P2 platform stability.
- [Services](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/astra/architecture/services/): 16 canonical microservices plus optional Slack adapter, webhook ingest, cost tracker.
- [Security](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/astra/security/): mTLS, JWT, RBAC, sandbox, Vault, approval system, dual-approval, super-admin redaction.
- [SLAs](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/astra/reference/slas/): 10ms p99 reads, 50ms median scheduling, 99.9% availability. Phases 0-10 complete.

## Wiki

- [Systems](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/systems/): Caching, streaming, consistency, observability reference notes.
- [Ruby & Rails](https://prashanthr.net/wiki/rails/): Philosophy, Active Record, Hotwire reference.

## Key facts

- Author: Prashanth Rajagopal, software architect with 15+ years building production systems
- Astra: autonomous agent OS — microkernel, actor model, DAG-based task graph, 16 microservices, chat agents, Slack integration
- Tech stack: Go (Astra kernel), Ruby/Rails, Python, TypeScript
- Interests: distributed systems, IoT, Home Assistant, RC cars, electronics, ESP32
- Site built with Zensical (MkDocs Material)
- Location: India
